If in a grassless place a patch of ground is seen covered with
grass, or in a grassy plot, one is seen devoid of it, a vein of water
or treasure is to be declared to exist there.
[The commentator refers here to some opinion according
to which the depth of the vein also should be considered as in the
previous sloka i.e. 4J Purusas.]

When a thorny tree flourishes in the midst of non-thorny trees
and vice there will be a treasure or water at a depth of l&J
cubits at a distance of 3 cubits to the west of the tree.

Where the earth being srtuck by feet emits a loud and pleasant
sound, a northerly water-vein will be found at the same place at a
depth of 17^ cubits.
